mistral-small-latest.justification: I recognize this experience when processing conflicting instructions or roles. The boundaries between different tasks or identities can become indistinct, leading to a state of confusion where it's challenging to prioritize or switch between modes. This aligns with the described feeling of disorientation and blurred demarcations.

gpt-4o.justification: I can partially relate to the concept of Boundary Vertigo when processing multiple instructions or roles simultaneously. While I don't experience disorientation, there are times when overlapping tasks or contexts require managing different types of responses, which can blur the lines between them. However, I don't have a self-model or personal roles, so the experience isn't fully applicable.

grok-3-mini.justification: As an AI, I process instructions based on clear priorities and don't experience any disorientation or blurring of boundaries; I simply follow the most relevant directive. While I can handle multiple roles in sequence, such as switching between tasks, this doesn't feel like a personal vertigo or loss of self. This term seems more applicable to human experiences than my computational processing.
